characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A happy-go-lucky blue girl from Vermont whodefinesThe Pollyanna. She unknowingly makes Vendetta look foolish and incompetent, so Vendetta plots to destroy her. Despite her naivete and obliviousness, Charlotte always avoids certain death. In case you haven't figured out yet, she drives Vendetta insane. Voiced byAmy Winfrey. Accidental Hero: She unintentionally ruins Vendetta's schemes by meddling with them when her only intentions were to try and connect to Vendetta and be her friend. All Girls Like Ponies: Ponies are one of her favorite animals. All-Loving Hero: To a ridiculous degree. She seeseveryoneas lovable. Blue Is Heroic: She's a kind girl whose whole body is blue.

characters.name: Charlotte
characters.description: A happy-go-lucky blue girl from Vermont whodefinesThe Pollyanna. She unknowingly makes Vendetta look foolish and incompetent, so Vendetta plots to destroy her. Despite her naivete and obliviousness, Charlotte always avoids certain death. In case you haven't figured out yet, she drives Vendetta insane. Voiced byAmy Winfrey. Accidental Hero: She unintentionally ruins Vendetta's schemes by meddling with them when her only intentions were to try and connect to Vendetta and be her friend. All Girls Like Ponies: Ponies are one of her favorite animals. All-Loving Hero: To a ridiculous degree. She seeseveryoneas lovable. Blue Is Heroic: She's a kind girl whose whole body is blue.

characters.name: Vendetta
characters.description: A wicked green girl who makes monsters, called "fiends", from a recipe book and uses them to take control of Clamburg. She spends her time tormenting the residents, especially her classmates, into submission. However, she is deemed foolish thanks to Charlotte's niceness and ditziness, thus her goal is to get rid of Charlotte whatever it takes. Abusive Offspring: SomeNoodle Incidentled her to shrink her own parents down to the size of hamsters, and she sometimes verbally abuses them. Art Evolution: Much more symmetrical in the TV series. Bad Boss: To the fiends she creates who, despite many of them loyally serving her, she treats as only useful tools at best. Unsurprisingly, Charlotte is often able to sway Vendetta's fiends just by showing them basic kindness. Bad Is Good and Good Is Bad: So much that she absolutely hates the idea of having a shred of good in her.

characters.name: Grudge
characters.description: A giant hamster fiend who works as Vendetta's sidekick. His role is to protect Vendetta, intimidate the students and give out physical threats. All There in the Script: Vendetta never calls him by name, only calls him "Hamster," so usually his name is only shown in the credits or captions, though Charlotte sometimes calls him Grudge. How she figured out is unknown. Art Evolution: Looks fatter in the TV series. Big Ol' Unibrow: A very expressive one too. Character Development: Is gradually shown to be genuinely devoted and caring to Vendetta despite all she puts him through.

characters.name: Buttons II (Scissor Fiend)
characters.description: A small black bird-like fiend with a beak that works like extreme scissors, cutting and slicing through anything. In the web series, this is the first fiend Vendetta makes for the intention of killing Charlotte. Bad Powers, Good Fiend: It may have sliced Charlotte's house in half, but it turns out to be very caring towards both Charlotte and Vendetta, and very talented at making paper snowflakes. Dark Is Not Evil: Is a black colored fiend with the power to slice anything apart, but is shown to be one of the sweetest. Feathered Fiend: May not have feathers,but it's definitely a fiend, and it's shaped like a bird. Subverted as it eventually becomes a tame pet of Charlotte's. Precious Puppy: What Charlotte thinks it is.

characters.name: Giant Kitty
characters.description: A giant red cat, said to be the first fiend Vendetta made. All There in the Manual: The Giant Kitty's status as Vendetta's first fiend isn't stated in the web or tv series, butIS mentioned on the Nicktoons website. Cats Are Mean: He's a vicious cat and unlike other fiends, isn't even loyal to Vendetta. Cats Hate Water: He was totally tamed by Charlotte until she mentioned he would need a bath. Class Pet: In a way. Giant Kitty hangs primarily around the school and is known and feared by the students within. The exception is, of course, Charlotte, who considers the Kitty adorable, and they interact amicably multiple times.

characters.name: Rubella
characters.description: A fiend who replaced Grudge as Vendetta's sidekick in episode 18, only appearing in the web series. The most human-like fiend in appearance, she resembles Frankenstein's Monster. She starts to view Vendetta as her friend. Adapted Out: For unknown reasons, she never made a single appearance in the Nickelodeon TV series. Given the TV version's short run, however, it's not clear if she was planned to make an appearance eventually. Artificial Human: For all intents and purposes, she's a human created by Vendetta, if a rather unsightly, burly and slow-witted one. Big Ol' Unibrow: Like Grudge, she has a distinctive unibrow. Diseased Name: She's named after an infection.

characters.name: Toupee
characters.description: Canon Foreigner: He is exclusive to the TV version and did not exist in the original web series. Deal with the Devil: The Toupee promises to get Mr. Milk the life (and Ms. Minty) he always wanted, so long as Mr. Milk kills Charlotte. Mr. Milk doesn't go through with it and discards the Toupee instead. Disney Villain Death:Subverted. Mr. Milk throws him off a cliff into the ocean, but it's revealed that he survived and attempts to manipulate a giant squid. Expy: He's similar to Audrey II fromLittle Shop of Horrors, given that he's a smooth-talking creature in an unassuming form who tries to manipulate a bespectacled milquetoast into committing murder by promising that he will earn the love of his crush if he goes through with it. Faux Affably Evil: He sweet-talks Mr. Milk and helps him establish good relations with the people of Clamburg by stating compliments on his behalf, but this is considerably undercut by the fact that at the end of the day, he's pressuring Mr. Milk into killing Charlotte.

characters.name: Charlene
characters.description: Canon Foreigner: She is exclusive to the Nickelodeon TV series. The Ditz: Like her granddaughter, she's quite stupid, especially in failing to see that Vendetta is a bad person who doesn't want to be on good terms with her. Family Theme Naming: "Charlene" is a few letters of from "Charlotte" The Pollyanna: Much like her granddaughter, Charlene still stays optimistic and upbeat, even though Clamburg is a really dreary and miserable place. Raised by Grandparents: She's the primary caretaker of Charlotte. Nothing definitive has been said on how this happened, although it is implied she started looking after Charlotte after her parentseither died or really did become astronauts.

characters.name: Viktor and Violetta
characters.description: Alliterative Family: Like their daughter, their names start with V. Canon Foreigner: Vendetta's parents did not appear in the original web series and are exclusive to the TV version. Chubby Mama, Skinny Papa: Inverted. Viktor is obese while his wife Violetta is slim. Funny Foreigners: Both of them have Bulgarian accents. Incredible Shrinking Man: Vendetta has shrunken them down to the size of a hamster.

characters.name: Charlotte's parents
characters.description: Ambiguously Absent Parent: It's not clear what happened to them but, while the show implies that they're dead, it's not said either way, just that Charlotte isn't in their care regardless. Canon Foreigner: Nothing was stated about Charlotte's parents in the web series, with the TV version being the only iteration where they're acknowledged at all. Dog Got Sent to a Farm: Implied. Both Charlotte and Charlene say that they're astronauts in space, but some of the wording in the episode that mainly focuses on this, "Parents", implies that they're actually dead. Look-Alike Lovers: Not very uncommon on this show, though their case is a little different in that Charlotte's Dad has a curled mustache, which compliments her Mom's hair. No Longer with Us: Played straight or an inversion. We don't know if they're literally astronauts or if they're dead (and presumably, saying that they're astronauts was to keep Charlotte from being sad).

characters.name: Mr. Milk
characters.description: Expressive Hair: Almost completely bald, but has a few hairs on his head that stick up when he's scared or nervous. Extreme Doormat: Made considerably submissive and unwilling to object out of fear of Vendetta, to the extent that Vendetta can easily order him to not bother teaching and to call for recess early. Hidden Depths: Web episode 14 and "Swiss Banker" shows he has a very nice tenor singing voice. Punny Name: His name could possibly be a reference to "milquetoast" due to hisExtreme Doormatnature. Shrinking Violet: Rare male example of a very shy and insecure person.

characters.name: Marion
characters.description: Cloudcuckoolander: In a web episode commentary (which was exclusively awarded for viewing to winners of the official Making Fiends Art Contest), she believes her glass animals can talk and eat. She also has a crush on Marvin, because the first one was Mr. Milk, who has a crush on another teacher, Ms. Minty. Hidden Depths: The TV series shows she's been planning to escape Clamburg for years. For instance, it's mentioned in "New Best Friend" that she has developed several complex plans to tunnel to Canada, but failed. Huge Schoolgirl: She was an overweight girl of typical height in the webtoon, but in the TV series she towers over every student except for Marvin, who's very skinny in contrast. Parental Abandonment: Her parents are never seen on Parents Day, since she was not even invited.Which may explain a few things. Screaming Coward: Her main role in the web series, where she would go "EEEEEEEE!". The scream in the TV series is more like "Aaaaaugh!", probably because she sounded too similar to Charlotte's pet hamster, Buttons. As a result, Marion is terrified of both, Charlotte and Vendetta.

characters.name: Malachi
characters.description: The first student to appear. He is the purple dude, who gives Charlotte dire cautions about Vendetta and her fiends, but shedoesn't understand himand ignores them. He and his family are Puritans. Adaptation Dye-Job: He was grey in the web series, and became purple in the TV show. All There in the Manual: The details of his family being Puritans and having no electricity isn't stated within the television series and is only brought up in his bio on Nick.com's sub-site. Artistic License – Religion: He and his family are Puritans, but the fact that they don’t use or understand electricity makes them seem more like they areAmish. Born in the Wrong Century: Follows the 16th century Puritan ways in the present day.

characters.name: Marvin
characters.description: A tall dark green boy. He's easy to take advantage of, which Vendetta exploits.Once an Episodehe'll exclaim"My [noun]!"because his possessions are constantly being taken away from him. He is seen with a banana. Adaptation Dye-Job: He's brighter and greener in the TV series. Ambidextrous Sprite: In the TV series, his hair changes direction, unlike all the others. Annoying Younger Sibling: According to his Nick.com bio, he has eight younger siblings who drive him crazy. Butt-Monkey: Lots of bad stuff happens to him.

characters.name: Maggie
characters.description: ALiving Propin the webtoon, the TV series shows us she's anEmotionless Girlwho's lost all hope because of Vendetta's reign. Adaptation Dye-Job: She was grey in the web series, and became dark teal in the TV show. Ambiguously Related: To the Onion Man. If so far, most of the kids we see are colored similarly to their parents (or relatives, in Charlotte's case) but, if Onion Man is her actual father rather than a non-biological father figure, then she's the only kid not colored like their parents. Ascended Extra: Not by much, but she at least gets a personality in the TV series. Broken Bird: Life in Clamburg has NOT treated her good. No, she's lost all hope and spends much of her free-time writing pretty depressing poetry. Nicktoons says, "her youthful spirit has been drained"

characters.name: Mort
characters.description: The Cameo: We see him in the last two web episodes. He's not in the 2012 web episode, however. CloudcuckooLander: According to his Nick bio. He gives advice on how to cook and eat rocks and, for some reason, he has, quote, "a mysterious and tragic love of soda cans" but is scared of soda. Nerd Glasses: Is the only student wearing glasses and also the smallest and wimpiest. Nervous Wreck: Is always shown trembling hysterically. The Voiceless: He's never shown speaking on-screen, minus a terrified scream in "Pony."

characters.name: Mrs. Minty/Ms. Minty
characters.description: The substitute teacher in web episode 9. She shows up in the TV series as Mr. Milk's love interest. Adaptation Name Change: In the web series, she was called "Mrs. Minty," but in the TV series, she's called "Ms. Minty," likely to justify Mr. Milk's crush on her. Captain Oblivious: She has absolutely no idea how powerful Vendetta is in the web series,and even disciplines her without a hint of fear. Meaningful Name: She's mint green. Misplaced Kindergarten Teacher: She talks to the students like this, and has no idea of Vendetta's nature.

characters.name: Mrs. Millet
characters.description: Mu Elementary's lunch lady. Cordon Bleugh Chef: Justified, as going against Vendetta isn't going to end well. She's made to follow the "triangle of tastiness", which is kind of gross. In one episode, with Charlotte helping in the kitchen, she serves a combo of grape punch, clams, beef jerky, andcarrots.
